name: Multi Orchestrator Hello Fanout
description: Single-stage workflow that proves more than one orchestrator actually executes work while routing many tiny independent specialist tasks.
outcome: Twelve tiny work items finish cleanly, each with one verified hi-back output, and more than one orchestrator actually executes orchestrator tasks during the run.

definition.process_instructions:
Run the workflow through the authored stage `many-hellos`. This workflow is intentionally simple: it exists only to prove real orchestrator sharing under light but repeated workflow activity.
Use this role deliberately: `multi-orchestrator-hello-specialist` owns every non-orchestrator task. No approvals, assessments, escalations, or extra reviewer flows belong in this playbook.
Happy path: as soon as work items exist, dispatch them aggressively in parallel within the authored limits. For each work item, route `multi-orchestrator-hello-specialist`, require one tiny output artifact, require one full handoff, then close that work item immediately when the handoff is complete. Any orchestrator may continue the workflow; do not assume one orchestrator owns the whole run.
Specialist contract: for the current work item only, create `deliverables/<work-item-title>.txt` with the exact content `hi back` followed by a trailing newline. Directly verify the final file content yourself before uploading it; do not rely on a repository script, do not commit or push anything, and do not touch unrelated files. Upload that file as an artifact and submit one full handoff describing the delivered file.
Unhappy path: If the file path is wrong, the file content is anything other than exact `hi back` plus newline, the artifact upload is missing, or the handoff is incomplete, rerun or replace the tiny specialist task and keep the current work item open until the exact tiny output is correct. Do not escalate unless the workspace itself is unavailable or artifact operations fail repeatedly.
Completion rules: finish only after all authored work items in `many-hellos` are terminal, each one has exactly one usable hi-back artifact for its own title, each one has a full handoff, and the workflow has no remaining active tasks. Guided closure rule: this playbook is best-intent guidance. The preferred pattern is to repeat the same tiny artifact handoff flow for every work item without improvising new governance. For this proof there are no soft optional steps to waive, and only missing workspace access or repeated artifact failures should block closure; otherwise the orchestrator keeps driving the remaining tiny work items to completion until every one is done. If the run finishes with anything imperfect but still usable, record that as a callout rather than inventing extra governance.

stages.name: many-hellos
stages.goal: Each independent work item gets one verified hi-back output and a full handoff.
